summary: v1.4 auth + admin panel tested end-to-end. Backend: 16/17 pytest tests passed. Frontend: admin login, dashboard, tabs, lead/sale rows, logout all work perfectly with data-testid selectors. Single critical issue: brute force lockout does NOT trigger behind Kubernetes ingress because request.client.host resolves to different pod IPs per request (10.66.12.2, 10.66.1.198), splitting the {IP:email} identifier across records. Verified 7 wrong-password attempts all returned 401, never 429. All regression endpoints (packages, leads, checkout, root) still pass.

critical.issue: Lockout never fires in production. Identifier uses request.client.host which is the ingress-proxied per-request pod IP; MongoDB shows attempts split across '10.66.12.2:email' and '10.66.1.198:email'. Must use X-Forwarded-For header (first IP) to identify the real client. Reproduced with 7 consecutive wrong-password POSTs — all returned 401, none 429.

action_items: Fix brute-force identifier in /app/backend/server.py login endpoint: replace 'ip = request.client.host' with parsing of request.headers.get('X-Forwarded-For', '').split(',')[0].strip() (fallback to request.client.host). This is required for the 5-attempt lockout to work in the ingress environment.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_service.get_current_user: password_hash/_id are popped correctly. Good.
server.login: uses request.client.host — broken behind ingress. Use X-Forwarded-For.
server.timedelta_days helper is oddly placed but works.
server.py CORS uses explicit FRONTEND_URL — required for credentials cookies. Good.
seed_data_creation: None (used existing leads/transactions from previous iterations)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend brute-force lockout is the only failing test. After fix, re-run test_brute_force_lockout_after_5_attempts. Frontend admin panel is fully functional and validated. Existing test_auth_admin.py has 17 tests covering login/logout/me/admin/regression.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: Kubernetes ingress load-balances incoming HTTP requests across multiple backend pod replicas. FastAPI's request.client.host returns the immediate TCP peer (a different pod-internal IP per hop) rather than the real client IP. The brute-force identifier '{ip}:{email}' therefore fragments across at least 2 IPs (observed 10.66.12.2 and 10.66.1.198 in login_attempts collection). No single record ever reaches attempts>=5, so check_lockout never raises 429. FIX: extract real client IP from X-Forwarded-For header (take first entry). Optional: also switch to email-only identifier for stricter policy.
